title: Hedge Funds Turn Most Bullish on Oil Since 2020 Amid Iran War
summary: Hedge funds turned the most bullish on Brent oil in six years at the outset of one of the crude market’s most volatile weeks ever.

title: US Allows Venezuela to Sell Fertilizers as War Crimps Supply
summary: The Trump administration authorized Venezuela to sell fertilizers and other petrochemicals to US companies, further loosening sanctions just as the Iran war tightens global supplies of critical crop nutrients.

title: Brent Crude Closes Above $100 for Second Straight Session
summary: Brent crude settled above $100 a barrel for the second straight session, ending the day at the highest level in more than three years as the conflict in the Middle East drags on and world leaders struggle to resolve the biggest disruption to the oil market in history.

Hims Shares Notch Record Week as Novo Pact Spurs Optimism
summary: Hims & Hers Health Inc. shares posted their best week on record as a new partnership with Novo Nordisk A/S fuels fresh optimism about the company’s future in weight-loss drugs.

title: EM Posts Weekly Slump as Oil Shock Triggers Rates Repricing
summary: Emerging-market assets recorded their second week of losses and traders repriced several monetary policy moves as the Middle East crisis shows no signs of abating and concerns grow over the impact of high oil prices.
